arts.sum: Lazio midfielder Matteo Guendouzi crowns his teammate Nuno Tavares as one of the best left-backs in the continent. The two players are good friends having played together in the past at Arsenal and then Olympique Marseille. arts.ttl: Juventus and Lazio Already Priced Out for Former Target
arts.sum: Mason Greenwood has thrived since transferring from Manchester United to Olympique Marseille, fueling the regrets of Juventus and Lazio, which had a chance to sign him last summer. He didn’t transfer for cheap, as the Ligue 1 side spent €26M. The post Juventus and Lazio Already Priced Out for Former Target appeared first on The Cult of Calcio.

arts.ttl: Europa League | Lazio – Nice: Match Preview
arts.sum: After kicking off their Europa League campaign in style, Lazio look to maintain momentum as they host Nice at the Stadio Olimpico on Thursday evening. The Italian side made a statement with a convincing 3-0 victory over Dynamo Kyiv on Matchday 1, while Nice were held to a 1-1 draw at home by Real Sociedad. As the two clubs meet in Rome, Lazio will be favored to secure another positive result and take control of Group E. Following an inconsistent start to the Serie A season, Lazio appear to be hitting their stride under new manager Marco Baroni. arts.ttl: Lazio president Lotito: “I was the first to fight against the ultras….”
arts.sum: Claudio Lotito, the president of Lazio, has come forward with a powerful narrative of his unwavering stance against ultras and his commitment to legality in football. In a recent interview with Il Messaggero (via Calciomercato), Lotito painted a vivid picture of his ongoing struggle against criminal elements in the sport. “I don’t want to appear heroic, especially at this moment, but I was the first to take a very clear position,” Lotito stated.
